title: ficus stephanocarpa var. subglabra
intro: Ficus stephanocarpa var. subglabra, also known as the Indian laurel or subglabrous fig, is a variety of fig tree native to India and Southeast Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties, which can be used to treat various health conditions. Ficus stephanocarpa var. subglabra has been reported to have several health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and treating skin conditions due to its antimicrobial properties. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and fruits are typically used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures that can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
